outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Zero tankers transit Hormuz as dual blockade brings world's most important oil chokepoint to standstill
outlets.summary: CNN reports zero tanker transits through the Strait of Hormuz on April 20 — one of the quietest days since the conflict began. The combination of the US CENTCOM blockade and Iran's re-closure has brought maritime traffic to a near-complete halt.

outlets.name: KPBS/NPR
outlets.headline: U.S.-Iran ceasefire expires this week with no deal in sight
outlets.summary: Analysis of the April 22 ceasefire expiration with no diplomatic framework in place. Iran has declined Round 2 Pakistan talks while US delegation heads to Islamabad anyway.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Iran says 3,500 dead in war against US as Hormuz talks stall
outlets.summary: Fox covers Iranian casualty claims and the stalling of Hormuz negotiations as ceasefire expiration approaches.

outlets.name: Daily Caller
outlets.headline: Trump Announces US Navy Seized Blockade-Defying Iranian Cargo Vessel After 'Blowing A Hole' In Engine Room
outlets.summary: Coverage of the USS Spruance seizing the Iranian cargo ship Touska after firing rounds into its engine room. Trump touted the blockade costs Iran $500M/day.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Does Israel's 'Yellow Line' violate the Lebanon ceasefire?
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera investigates the IDF's establishment of a 10km military zone in southern Lebanon during ceasefire, with systematic demolition of 1,400+ buildings and 55 villages blocked.

outlets.name: Euronews
outlets.headline: Hormuz standoff reignites as IRGC appears to now shape Iran's decisions
outlets.summary: Analysis of how IRGC has effectively taken control of Iran's diplomatic and military posture, overriding moderate voices like FM Araghchi.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: Like Gaza: IDF Razes South Lebanon Villages During Cease-fire, Sources Say
outlets.summary: Haaretz reports IDF is systematically demolishing civilian homes in southern Lebanon during the ceasefire, comparing operations to Gaza. Paid contractors using heavy equipment.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: IDF confirms photo of soldier destroying Jesus statue in Lebanon village is real, vows action
outlets.summary: IDF confirms authenticity of viral photo showing soldier smashing Jesus statue in Debel. Northern Command investigation launched. International outrage growing.

outlets.name: IRNA (via ProtoThema)
outlets.headline: No second round of talks with the US — Washington's demands are excessive
outlets.summary: Iran's official news agency formally declined Round 2 negotiations citing excessive demands, unrealistic expectations, constant shifts, repeated contradictions, and ongoing blockade.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Enough with the lecturing — Iran tells EU its Hormuz stance is 'epitome of hypocrisy'
outlets.summary: Iran FM spokesperson Baghaei fires back at EU's Kallas over Hormuz stance, calling it the 'epitome of hypocrisy' and demanding an end to Western lectures on international law.
